scope.summary: Rust block_on reporting now follows same-block local variables whose last visible assignment is proof-backed tokio runtime or handle construction.

capability_alignment.capability: runtime receiver provenance for future drive scheduling attribution
not_a_feature_list: No exact admission path was opened for Rust block_on or async/sync equivalence.
No new public semantic-pack API was added.
Function parameters, struct fields such as self.rt, and unknown local variables remain closed.
Runtime construction through map_err(...)? remains closed until callback/effect obligations are modeled.
reused_capabilities: shared future-drive scheduling and future-settled value-channel obligations

new_reporting_surfaces.guard: Handle must be qualified or imported from tokio::runtime, unshadowed, and the tokio root must not be project-local.
Runtime must be qualified or imported from tokio::runtime, unshadowed, and the local binding must be the last visible assignment before the block_on receiver.

new_reporting_surfaces.surface: let rt = Builder::new_*().<allowed builder chain>.build().unwrap()/expect(...); rt.block_on(...)
new_reporting_surfaces.guard: Builder must be qualified or imported from tokio::runtime, unshadowed, and only root-preserving builder-chain methods are accepted.

new_reporting_surfaces.surface: Runtime::new()?.block_on(...), let rt = Runtime::new()?; rt.block_on(...), and let rt = Builder::new_*().build()?; rt.block_on(...)
new_reporting_surfaces.guard: The Rust ? operator is accepted only as source-backed TryPropagation directly over a proven tokio runtime result expression.

assertions: Local Handle::current binding reports future-drive and future-settled obligations.
Local Runtime::new().unwrap binding reports future-drive and future-settled obligations.
A parent-block Runtime binding remains visible to a nested block_on receiver.
Local Builder::new_current_thread().build().unwrap binding reports future-drive and future-settled obligations.
Direct ? over Runtime::new() or Builder::build() preserves runtime receiver provenance.
A local binding whose RHS wraps the runtime constructor stays closed.
A local binding reassigned to a non-runtime value before block_on stays closed.
An inner-block shadow of a proven runtime binding stays closed.
A field assignment whose field name matches the local receiver does not prove that receiver.
A nested-block runtime binding does not prove an out-of-scope receiver.
A local binding through Runtime::new().map_err(...)? stays closed.

current_crates_gate.note: The local crates surface has no proof-backed Rust local runtime block_on bridge rows.

summary.primary_reason_note: The unit also carries async-await evidence, which remains the primary scheduling subreason by rule order.
The matching block_on units also carry async-block evidence, which remains the primary scheduling subreason by rule order.

closed_surfaces.reason: The reporter does not infer struct-field tokio runtime types.

closed_surfaces.surface: function parameters such as fn run(rt: Runtime) { rt.block_on(...) }
closed_surfaces.reason: Parameter type identity is not modeled for Rust runtime receiver provenance in this slice.

closed_surfaces.surface: runtime construction hidden behind wrapper calls
closed_surfaces.reason: Wrapper calls can change receiver type or scheduling semantics.
The map_err callback introduces callback/effect obligations beyond direct TryPropagation over a runtime result.

closed_surfaces.surface: Exact convergence between block_on, await, and synchronous payload values
closed_surfaces.reason: Future drive scheduling, wake/poll behavior, cancellation/liveness, result-channel, and receiver/type identity contracts remain missing evidence.

next_exact_work_policy.summary: Rust local runtime block_on bridges are now attributable, but exact async/sync recovery still needs poll/wake scheduling, cancellation/liveness, result-channel, callback/effect, and broader receiver/type identity contracts.
